questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: During an adiabatic process, the density of a gas is found to be proportional to cube of temperature. The degree of freedom of gas molecule is
questions.solution:
For adiabatic process, $\mathrm{TV}^{\nu-1}=$ constant
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{T}\left(\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\rho}\right)^{\gamma-1}=\text { constant } \\
& \frac{\mathrm{T}}{\rho^{r-1}}=\text { constant } \\
& \rho \propto \mathrm{T}^{\gamma /(\gamma-1)} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{\gamma-1}=3 \Rightarrow \gamma=4 / 3 \\
& f=\frac{2}{\gamma-1}=\frac{2}{\left(\frac{4}{3}-1\right)}=6
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two cylinders $A$ and $B$ fitted with pistons contain equal amounts of an ideal diatomic gas at 300K. The piston $A$ is free to move, while that of $B$ is held fixed. The same amount of heat is given to the gas in each cylinder. If the rise in temperature of the gas in A is 30 K , then the rise in temperature of the gas in $B$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{ll}\text { for A } & \text { for B }\\
{d Q}=n c_p d T & {d Q}=n c_v d T^{\prime} \\ n c_p d T  =n c d T^{\prime} \\ \Rightarrow d T  =\frac{c_p}{c_v} \times 30 = 42K\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
